sector.name: Internet & Platforms
sector.description: Digital platforms scaling growth and profitability

fyPerformance: FY26 GMV and net revenue grew 28% and 26% respectively, with net revenue crossing 10,000 Cr.
Q4FY26 net revenue grew 28% YoY to 2,648 Cr, while EBITDA rose 67% YoY to 223 Cr.
Beauty remained the core growth engine with FY26 GMV near 15,000 Cr and EBITDA margin of 9.6%.
Gross profit grew 30% YoY to 4,516 Cr with margin at 45.1%.
PAT improved sharply to around 204 Cr, showing better scale-led profitability.

guidance.value: Scale owned brands and beauty platform profitably

highlights: Gross profit grew 30% YoY in FY26 with gross margin at 45.1%.
PAT margin improved to around 2% of net revenue for FY26.
Dot & Key and Kay Beauty continued to scale as key owned brands.
Retail footprint reached 313 stores across 99 cities.
House of Nykaa GMV grew around 50% YoY to 3,176 Cr.
Owned brands supported both growth and margin expansion.
FSN E-Commerce Ventures Ltd.quote: "Growth remained broad-based across beauty, fashion and owned brands."

fyPerformance: Management reiterated a medium-term path toward $1 Bn EBITDA by FY29.
Quick commerce remains in expansion mode, with 60% NOV CAGR targeted over three years.
Food delivery is expected to grow 18-20% over the medium term with steady-state EBITDA margin of around 5%.
Dark store expansion remains a major growth driver for quick commerce.
Management maintained confidence in medium-term quick commerce margin potential.
highlights: Quick commerce store expansion remains the main investment area.
Management indicated QC margins can move toward 3-3.5% over time.
Food delivery remains a more mature profit pool within the platform.
The company is targeting 3,000 dark stores by March.
FY27 quick commerce growth may moderate from earlier high-growth expectations.
Integrated food and quick commerce scale remains the key platform advantage.
Eternal Ltd.quote: "The medium-term growth and profitability ambition remains intact."

fyPerformance: Food delivery continues to be guided for 18-20% medium-term growth.
Management sees steady-state food delivery EBITDA margin potential of around 5%.
Quick commerce remains in investment mode, but contribution margin discipline is improving.
Management highlighted quick commerce contribution margin breakeven as an important milestone.
Platform initiatives remain focused on category expansion and better customer frequency.
highlights: Management emphasized profitable growth instead of chasing low-quality volume.
Quick commerce contribution margin breakeven was highlighted as an important milestone.
Platform innovation and category expansion remain key growth levers.
Food delivery remains the more mature earnings base.
Quick commerce ambition stands at 1 Lakh Cr GOV over 3.5-5 years.
Management expects growth to be balanced with improving contribution discipline.
Swiggy Ltd.quote: "The focus is on growth with improving contribution discipline."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 19% while adjusted EBITDA rose sharply to 559 Cr.
PAT stood at 113 Cr, supported by margin expansion and operating leverage.
Full-year payment volume was close to $200 Bn across around 2 Mn touchpoints.
Adjusted EBITDA margin improved by around 500 bps through FY26.
Operating cash flow improved meaningfully, with Q4 operating cash flow at 676 Cr.
highlights: Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded by around 500 bps.
Q4 operating cash flow stood at 676 Cr.
Issuing and acquiring businesses continue to contribute meaningfully to revenue.
Management guided FY27 revenue growth at 21-23.5%.
Contribution margin remained strong at around 74-75%.
Capex intensity is expected to remain controlled.
Pine Labs Ltd.quote: "Revenue growth is expected to accelerate with better EBITDA and cash flow."

fyPerformance: Management expects FY27 revenue growth to accelerate across payments, marketing services and financial services.
Q4 GMV grew 27% YoY, with core net payment margin above 4 bps.
FY26 EBITDA margin ended around 6%, creating a base for operating leverage.
Merchant GMV growth remained healthy across offline and online use cases.
Management expects indirect cost growth to remain below revenue and contribution profit growth.

guidance.value: Expected below revenue and contribution profit growth

highlights: AI-led efficiencies are expected to control indirect cost growth.
Merchant GMV trends remain healthy across offline and online channels.
Financial services and marketing services are expected to support revenue acceleration.
Medium-term EBITDA margin aspiration remains 15-20%.
Management indicated a 2.5-3 year path toward margin targets.
Core net payment margin improved above earlier guidance.
One97 Communications Ltd.quote: "Operating leverage should improve as revenue scales faster than indirect costs."

fyPerformance: FY26 consolidated NTV grew 33% YoY to 4,290 Cr.
Q4 revenue grew 43% YoY to 426 Cr, while Q4 consolidated NTV grew 42%.
International business grew 75% for the full year and turned adjusted EBITDA positive.
India Consumer Services generated 131 Cr adjusted EBITDA for FY26.
The transacting user base grew 24% to 8.4 Mn.
highlights: India Consumer Services full-year adjusted EBITDA stood at 131 Cr.
83% of NTV came from retained users.
Native losses narrowed meaningfully as scale improved.
International NTV reached 700 Cr for FY26.
Management targets consolidated adjusted EBITDA breakeven by Q3FY28.
FY31 adjusted EBITDA ambition stands at 1,000 Cr.
Urban Company Ltd.quote: "The company is investing for growth while keeping a clear path to breakeven."

fyPerformance: FY26 insurance premium grew 42% to 29,934 Cr, with new protection premium up 57%.
Operating revenue grew 37% to 6,794 Cr, while Q4 operating revenue was nearly 2,000 Cr.
PAT stood at 670 Cr, showing strong operating leverage across the core business.
Q4 insurance premium grew 46% YoY, led by strong protection demand.
UAE business grew 54% YoY and continued to add a new growth vector.

guidance.value: Expected to be meaningfully EBITDA positive

highlights: Q4 insurance premium grew 46% YoY.
Core online insurance premium grew 39% for the year.
UAE business grew 54% YoY.
New protection premium grew 67% YoY in Q4.
Paisabazaar turned EBITDA positive in Q4.
Management continues to guide around 30% growth while aiming to outperform.
PB Fintech Ltd.quote: "The business continues to compound with strong premium growth and operating leverage."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 35% to 3,900 Cr, while EBITDA rose around 3x to 300 Cr.
Q4 revenue grew 51% YoY to 919 Cr.
PAT loss narrowed sharply to 24 Cr, with management highlighting underlying profitability after one-off adjustments.
Paid enrolments crossed 10 lakh new enrolments, up 20% YoY.
Online revenue grew 39% and reached 50.1% of total revenue.
highlights: Online revenues grew 39% and reached 50.1% of revenue.
PBT turned positive at 10 Cr versus a loss last year.
Paid enrolment growth remained strong across the platform.
Management targets full-year PAT profitability in FY27.
Offline business is expected to break even in FY27.
Online revenue mix is targeted to move toward 55% over three years.
PhysicsWallah Ltd.quote: "FY27 is expected to be the year of full-year PAT profitability."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 19.5% to 27.1 Bn, with EBITDA margin expanding to 22.5%.
Q4 revenue and EBITDA both grew 20.3% YoY.
The company delivered its 13th consecutive quarter of sequential topline growth.
PAT grew 19.1% to 4.55 Bn with PAT margin at 16.3%.
Operating cash flow remained strong at 5.02 Bn for FY26.
highlights: FY26 operating cash flow stood at 5.02 Bn.
CPCU conversions reached 120.3 Mn in Q4 at a CPCU rate of 60.
Direct advertisers contributed around 75% of business.
EBITDA margin expanded by around 120 bps in FY26.
Patent portfolio reached 18 granted patents with 21 filed or pending.
Medium-term revenue CAGR guidance remains around 20%.
Affle (India) Ltd.quote: "Affle continues to target 20% CAGR with disciplined profitability."

fyPerformance: FY26 total income grew around 55% to 715 Cr.
Adjusted EBITDA rose to around 190 Cr, while PAT turned positive at 160 Cr.
Q4 adjusted EBITDA grew 30% YoY to 50.2 Cr.
Tolling GTV grew 27% YoY, outperforming the broader NETC CV market.
Free cash flow from operations remained strong at around 185-190 Cr.

guidance.value: Expected to exit investment mode by FY27-end

highlights: Transacting customers grew around 13% YoY.
Free cash flow from operations stood around 185-190 Cr.
Profitability turnaround continued from deep losses to full-year PAT profit.
Power users grew around 22% YoY.
Vehicle finance is expected to exit investment mode by FY27-end.
The core business delivered strong cash conversion.
Zinka Logistics Solutions Ltd.quote: "The platform delivered scale growth with a sharp profitability turnaround."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 69.4% YoY to 1,824 Cr, ahead of revised guidance after the Sojern acquisition.
Q4FY26 revenue reached an all-time high of 716 Cr, with organic RateGain revenue up 19.3% YoY.
Adjusted PAT stood at 249.9 Cr for FY26, while reported PAT stood at 194.4 Cr.
Adjusted EBITDA margin stood at 23.5% in Q4FY26.
Annualized revenue run rate crossed 2,850 Cr.
highlights: Annualized revenue run rate crossed 2,850 Cr.
Sojern integration is ahead of plan, with annualized cost synergies at $15 Mn.
Management expects free cash flow to EBITDA conversion above 75% in FY27.
FY27 revenue guidance stands at 3,000-3,100 Cr.
FY27 EBITDA guidance stands at 650-700 Cr.
Management aims to build a $1 Bn revenue company by FY30-FY31.
RateGain Travel Technologies Ltd.quote: "The platform is built, the data is unmatched and the opportunity ahead is the largest in our history."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 22%, while EBITDA grew 70% with EBITDA margin at 33%.
Q4 EBITDA grew 55% and Q4 EBITDA margin expanded to 35%.
FY26 PAT grew 68% to 243 Cr, supported by strong operating leverage.
Cash reserves increased to 1,244 Cr after adding around 300 Cr during the year.
Consumer, Remarketing and OLX all delivered strong profit growth.
highlights: Consumer group FY26 revenue grew 30% and EBITDA grew 96%.
Remarketing FY26 revenue grew 22% with EBITDA margin of 28%.
OLX FY26 EBITDA grew 54% and PAT grew 77%.
Three-year PAT CAGR stood at 82%.
Management targets PAT of 1,000 Cr over 4-5 years.
Cash reserves provide flexibility for organic and inorganic growth.
CarTrade Tech Ltd.quote: "The company is targeting a much larger profit pool over the next 4-5 years."
